hard.next_step: June 12 Rick: Exec Champion says they've completed the Medix sales cycle for Direct, next up is Indirect. Miscommunication between Indirect champion (Marshall) and Exec Champion (Chris Bratt) - they need to meet internally today, figure out how they want to proceed.
hard.ais_why:
• Multiple Nexteer buyers pulled the conversation toward AI themselves. Global Purchasing Director Chris Bratt asked: "do the vendors engage in an AI conversation quite openly, or does it turn them off?" Call notes record that the autonomous AI negotiation and AP Smartdesk capabilities impressed the attendees, and Zycus was actioned to send AI negotiation case studies to Chris.
• The buyer also articulated AI-specific need language: enhanced automation for supplier onboarding, risk monitoring, and end-to-end sourcing event management, including autonomous negotiation for ad hoc purchases. Zycus has AI flagged as Very Critical for the account, and the in-scope product list explicitly includes Agentic AI.
• Why not Hungry: BANT is incomplete with budget still being built (a protected number for next year), Krzysztof Mekwinski's deepest concerns were ERP/QAD integration and three-way match, and Karolina Cisek's questions focused on invoice OCR and cycle times. With no AI POC committed yet, this sits at the cautious end of AI Curious.

north_star_verdict.headline: Momentum has reversed: the deal regressed from Shortlisted to Qualified and was cut to Pipeline on 9 Jun 2026, Direct went to Medix, and the Indirect play is stalled on an unresolved internal Marshall Bonner / Chris Bratt sequencing conflict with no buyer working session since 14 Nov 2025.
north_star_verdict.math: Close date 2026-11-20 is 137 days out (from 2026-07-06). Created and qualified originally 25 Mar 2025; the deal has sat in Qualified with no submission-date milestones logged, so stage velocity is effectively zero. On 9 Jun 2026 the stage was pushed backward (Shortlisted to Qualified) and the forecast cut (Upside Key Deal to Pipeline). Last real buyer touch was a social lunch with Chris Bratt on 14-15 May 2026 (52 days ago); the 12 Jun 2026 next step is a rep note of an unresolved internal Nexteer blocker with no buyer reply since. To hit 20 Nov the buyer must still run Indirect ShoeFit/use-case validation, commercials, ROI sign-off, negotiation and contracting; at current pace the date is not credible, but the primary problem is stalled momentum, not just timing.
evidence: Field history 2026-06-09: StageName Shortlisted to Qualified and ForecastCategoryName Upside Key Deal to Pipeline (by Rick Taranek) - a regression, not progress.

Next Step 2026-06-12: 'they've completed the Medix sales cycle for Direct, next up is Indirect. Miscommunication between Indirect champion (Marshall) and Exec Champion (Chris Bratt) - they need to meet internally today.'
LastActivityDate 2026-05-15 was a social lunch ('great to catch-up') with Chris Bratt, not a working session; no transcribed buyer call since 14 Nov 2025.

Economic Buyer Jacky Xu (VP, CPO) has never appeared on a call; Chris Bratt 10 Sep 2025: 'he'll be looking for me and my team to make a recommendation, and at that stage I think he'll start to engage.'

metrics.narrative: The value case is quantified but still Zycus-owned rather than buyer-validated. Arthur (Zycus value-engineering VP) sized roughly $4-5M year-one savings on tail spend alone against about $500M indirect spend on the 1 Aug 2025 and 14 Nov 2025 value-engineering calls, and Chris Bratt confirmed Nexteer has no single global system, with each region pulling spend differently ('every region has its Power BIs...but it just might be in a slightly different format for each region', 10 Sep 2025). The gap is that Nexteer never returned the region-by-region spend extract Arthur repeatedly requested, so the ROI remains a modeled estimate, not a jointly built number the buyer co-owns. Getting the buyer to return the spend extract and validate the savings model is the move that turns Metrics from partial to confirmed.
sources: Value Engineering call 2025-09-10 (Chris Bratt on regional spend data)
Value Engineering call 2025-11-14 and Level-set call 2025-08-01 ($4-5M savings)

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is mapped but has never engaged. Jacky Xu (VP, Chief Purchasing Officer, Global Supply Management) is the Executive Sponsor on the Salesforce contact roles and is the budget owner, but he has not appeared on a single call in 15+ months. Chris Bratt described the sequencing on 10 Sep 2025: 'he'll be looking for me and my team to make a recommendation, and at that stage I think he'll start to engage', and added 'I think Jackie's a believer... quite visionary when it comes to this sort of stuff.' Budget itself is a 'protected number for next year' still being built (AIS_Why__c). So authority is named and warm by proxy, but no direct EB access exists and none of the pricing conversations reached him; securing Chris's introduction to Jacky Xu once the team recommendation is ready is the move to close this.
sources: Value Engineering call 2025-09-10 (Chris Bratt on Jacky Xu)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are well documented from the 2025 workshops. Nexteer wants one global system to replace regional Power BI silos and consistent spend visibility (Chris Bratt, 10 Sep 2025), autonomous / AI-driven tail-spend negotiation and OCR-based invoice automation ('since we are gonna be focusing on the AI capabilities to automatically negotiate', Ronit, 1 Aug 2025), and multilingual support (English, Polish, Chinese, Spanish; Alexa and April, 25 Jun 2025). Eric Chavez set an explicit fit test on 14 Nov 2025 for a deep-dive functional demo and configurability for gaps. On Direct materials the criteria (APQP, PPAP, cost-recovery derivations) fell outside Zycus's platform, which Arthur conceded on 14 Nov 2025 ('that's simply not our space'), so the confirmed criteria that Zycus can win on are Indirect standardization, AI negotiation, and invoice/supplier automation.
sources: Value Engineering call 2025-09-10 (global system, spend visibility)
Level-set call 2025-08-01 (AI auto-negotiation of tail spend)

decision_process.narrative: The process is a two-track buy that has split. Direct materials ran its own selection and completed a Medix (Siemens Mendex) sales cycle (Next Step 12 Jun 2026); Indirect is the remaining Zycus play. The internal approval path is: Chris Bratt's team makes a recommendation, then Jacky Xu (VP, CPO) engages to sponsor and fund it (Chris Bratt, 10 Sep 2025). The budget is targeted for the next-year cycle, described as needing to be locked around August in the 1 Aug 2025 discussion. The current blocker is procedural rather than technical: the 12 Jun 2026 next step records a miscommunication between Marshall Bonner (Indirect champion) and Chris Bratt (exec champion) on how to sequence Indirect, and they needed to meet internally to decide how to proceed. Until that internal alignment and the team recommendation land, the process cannot advance to the EB.
sources: Next Step 2026-06-12 (Medix Direct complete; Marshall/Chris sequencing)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet visible because the deal is back at Qualified and no commercials are locked. What is known: Nexteer's legal is generally in-house with a lawyer assigned to the relevant stakeholder, and regional counsel sits in the US (Jonas and Pete), Poland, China and Mexico (Marshall Bonner, 10 Sep 2025), which signals a multi-region legal review at contracting. A single North America purchase order was the assumed commercial vehicle with internal cost allocation by region (Marshall, 25 Jun 2025). No MSA/SOW, InfoSec review, or procurement channel has been scoped. What would create visibility is a mutual close plan tied to the next-year budget lock that names the paper owner, the InfoSec path, and the regional legal reviewers.
sources: Zycus Users call 2025-06-25 (single NA PO, internal allocation)

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is specific and buyer-articulated by the relevant stakeholder has no global spend visibility (regional Power BI silos, no spend cube), pervasive maverick spot-buy behavior with no catalog contracts ('almost everything here is a spot buy...if you want a widget, you get a quote', 10 Sep 2025), and a broken invoice-status loop where suppliers get no acknowledgement ('there is no reliable communication path...the supplier does not know that his invoice was accepted, he only knows if it's rejected', 10 Sep 2025), forcing buyers to manually field payment-status queries. Roughly $500M of indirect spend is where Zycus flagged the fastest leverage. The pain owner is the procurement organization under Chris Bratt, with the relevant stakeholder carrying the operational detail; the inferred financial pain is the unmanaged tail spend Zycus sized at $4-5M year-one savings.
sources: Value Engineering call 2025-09-10 (spot buy, invoice-status gap)

champion.narrative: There are two champions, both operationally bought in but cooling. Marshall Bonner (Procurement Manager, SF Evaluator) is the Indirect champion who drove the supplier/invoicing demo asks, the ROI transparency requirement and the user-count/data-gathering work across 2025, praised Merlin as 'exceptional' (7 Jul 2025), and is named as the Indirect champion in the 12 Jun 2026 next step. Chris Bratt (Global Commodity and Product Line Purchasing Director, SF Decision Maker) is the exec champion and quarterback with EB access. What is missing is active advocacy: neither has introduced Jacky Xu, Chris has not scheduled the promised EB meeting in 9+ months, and the 12 Jun 2026 miscommunication between the two on Indirect sequencing shows they are not aligned. A strong champion would have resolved the sequencing question and escalated to the EB by now; rebuilding Marshall's and Chris's active advocacy ranks before any commercial step.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Marshall L. Bonner (Evaluator), Chris Bratt (Decision Maker)

competition.narrative: The live competitive threat is Medix (referred to as 'Mendex', a Siemens solution) plus the risk of deferral. Nexteer completed the Medix sales cycle for Direct materials (Next Step 12 Jun 2026), which is acceptable on its own because Zycus self-disqualified from Direct on APQP/PPAP (Arthur, 14 Nov 2025), but the 15 May 2026 lunch note is the real risk: 'they plan to use Mendex's Supplier module for all direct/indirect suppliers.' That intent lets Medix creep into the Indirect scope Zycus is counting on. The second threat is status-quo/deferral: Eric Chavez said Indirect is 'more like a year away, tied to our ERP activities' (14 Nov 2025). Zycus wins by making Indirect a parallel quick-win ($4-5M year-one tail-spend savings) with Merlin AI auto-negotiation and true global standardization that a Direct-materials supplier module cannot match, decoupled from the Direct/ERP timeline.
sources: Task 2026-05-15 (Mendex supplier module for all suppliers)
Direct Materials call 2025-11-14 (Zycus disqualified from Direct; Indirect deferral)

deal_movement.summary: The pulse is negative and slowing. Since the last sweep the deal formally regressed (Shortlisted to Qualified, Upside Key Deal to Pipeline on 9 Jun 2026), Direct materials was lost to Medix, and the only buyer contact was a social lunch (14-15 May 2026) plus a rep note of an unresolved internal blocker (12 Jun 2026). No transcribed buyer working session since 14 Nov 2025.

items.change: Stage regressed Shortlisted to Qualified and forecast cut Upside Key Deal to Pipeline (by Rick Taranek).
Next step logs Medix Direct sales cycle complete and an unresolved Marshall Bonner / Chris Bratt Indirect sequencing miscommunication.
Social lunch with Chris Bratt ('great to catch-up'); buyer intends to use Mendex supplier module for all direct/indirect suppliers.
Close date pushed 2026-06-30 to 2026-11-20 (by Rick Taranek).

competitive_position.summary: Medix (Mendex by Siemens) is the single strongest current threat: it won Nexteer's Direct materials selection (Next Step 12 Jun 2026) and the buyer stated on 15 May 2026 it plans to use Mendex's supplier module 'for all direct/indirect suppliers', which directly encroaches on Zycus's remaining Indirect wedge. Zycus is not competing on Direct (self-disqualified on APQP/PPAP, Arthur 14 Nov 2025), so the fight is Indirect + global standardization + AI. The second threat is status-quo/deferral, with Eric Chavez placing Indirect 'a year away, tied to ERP activities' (14 Nov 2025). Earlier unnamed S2P vendors evaluated for Direct (Eric 14 Nov 2025) are now dormant for Zycus's scope.

competitors.name: Medix (Mendex by Siemens)
competitors.quote: They are implementing a solution by Siemens called 'Mendex' for Direct Proc, they plan to use Mendex's Supplier module for all direct/indirect suppliers.
competitors.source: Task 2026-05-15 (Lunch with Chris Bratt) + Next Step 2026-06-12
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus Indirect plus Merlin AI tail-spend auto-negotiation and true global standardization as capabilities a Direct-materials supplier module cannot match; decouple Indirect from the Direct/ERP timeline as a $4-5M quick win.

competitors.name: Status-quo / Indirect deferral
competitors.quote: Indirect more like a year away, tied to our ERP activities.
competitors.how_we_win: Prove Indirect ROI is operationally independent of Direct/ERP and that deferral forfeits immediate tail-spend savings; secure a dated Indirect evaluation plan.

competitors.name: Unnamed S2P vendors (Direct evaluation)
competitors.quote: I've been trying to ask for a deep dive demo...between two and 4 hours with the other vendors that we're looking at.
competitors.how_we_win: Not applicable to Zycus scope (Direct); keep focus on Indirect where Zycus is differentiated.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on the Indirect criteria Zycus can win (global standardization, AI negotiation, invoice/supplier automation) and an explicit misfit on Direct materials that Zycus already conceded. ERP/QAD integration and multilingual coverage are partially validated and need confirmation.

items.criterion: One global procurement system to replace regional Power BI / spend silos
items.quote: What we don't have, which is why we're talking, is one global system where we pull all of that from in a consistent way.

items.criterion: AI / autonomous negotiation of unmanaged tail spend
items.quote: Since we are gonna be focusing on the AI capabilities to automatically negotiate, that spend will be automatically catered by AI.

items.criterion: Direct materials PPAP / APQP / cost-recovery workflow
items.quote: PPAP, even tracking the 18 docs, creating the living workflow, not a play for us. It's not part of the platform.

items.criterion: ERP / QAD integration and two-way / three-way invoice match
items.quote: For the US and Mexico, we use two way match. The rest of the world uses 3 way match.
But English, Polish, Chinese, and Spanish, and anything else that you wanna throw at us... Polish is something that we will check.
he'll be looking for me and my team to make a recommendation, and at that stage I think he'll start to engage.
items.source: Value Engineering call 2025-09-10 (Chris Bratt on Jacky Xu)

items.area: Region-by-region spend extract for ROI validation
items.quote: Chris, if I could ask you to do that, that'll help an awful lot... I'll take indirect and direct if you have it.
That's more likely gonna be more tied to our ERP activities, so more like a year away, honestly.

best_practice_check.summary: The win now hinges on restarting a stalled, regressed deal: convert the champions into active EB sponsorship, defend the Indirect scope from Medix, and turn the Zycus-modeled ROI into a buyer-owned number.
flags: Convert Chris Bratt from passive gatekeeper to active sponsor: the deal has stalled 15+ months and Jacky Xu (VP, CPO) has never engaged despite Chris's standing promise to bring him in after a team recommendation.

Defend the Indirect wedge before Medix absorbs it: Nexteer intends to use the Mendex supplier module 'for all direct/indirect suppliers' (15 May 2026), so Zycus must prove Indirect + Merlin AI value a Direct-materials module cannot match.
Make the $4-5M ROI buyer-owned: it is still a Zycus model because Nexteer never returned the regional spend extract; co-building it with Marshall and April is the fastest path to re-qualify the deal.

items.name: Jacky Xu
items.title: VP, Chief Purchasing Officer, Global Supply Management
items.risk: Deal cannot advance to funding or sign-off without EB engagement; no EB meeting in 15+ months despite Chris Bratt's promise.
items.sentiment: Unknown (never engaged; described by Chris Bratt as a believer/visionary)

items.name: Chris Bratt
items.title: Global Commodity and Product Line Purchasing Director
items.risk: Gates EB access and has not resolved the Indirect-sequencing conflict with Marshall (Next Step 12 Jun 2026).
items.sentiment: Neutral / cooling (bought in and socially engaged, but passive on EB escalation)

items.name: Marshall L. Bonner
items.title: Procurement Manager
items.risk: Indirect champion but not actively advocating; sequencing miscommunication with Chris unresolved.
items.sentiment: Neutral / cooling (operationally bought in, praised Merlin, quiet on working sessions since late 2025)

items.name: Eric Chavez
items.title: Global Systems Manager (Supply Chain)
items.risk: Technical/systems gatekeeper who frames Indirect as ERP-dependent, risking deferral.
items.sentiment: Neutral (prioritizes Direct replatforming; sees Indirect as a year away)

items.name: April Bladecki
items.title: QAD Global Supply Management Subject Matter Expert
items.risk: Owns QAD spend/user data needed for ROI and integration scoping; must stay engaged.
items.sentiment: Neutral (data/systems SME on user counts and QAD integration)

items.name: Krzysztof Mekwinski
items.title: Enterprise Architect
items.risk: Integration gatekeeper for QAD / potential SAP HANA; concerns must be validated before contracting.
items.sentiment: Unknown (ERP/QAD integration and three-way-match concerns per AIS_Why)

items.name: Karolina Cisek
items.title: EMEA Indirect Purchasing Manager
items.risk: Regional buy-in weak if excluded from ROI modeling and demos.
items.sentiment: Unknown (EMEA regional Indirect stakeholder; OCR/cycle-time focus per AIS_Why)

champion_strength.summary: Marshall L. Bonner (Procurement Manager) is the operational Indirect champion and Chris Bratt (Decision Maker) the exec champion/quarterback, both bought in during 2025 but now cooling: no active EB introduction, no working session since late 2025, and an unresolved Marshall/Chris sequencing miscommunication (12 Jun 2026). Advocacy is passive; strength is developing at best and at risk of decay without re-engagement.
champion_strength.champion: Marshall L. Bonner (Indirect champion); Chris Bratt (exec champion)
champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole; Next Step 2026-06-12; Budget call 2025-07-07; Value Engineering call 2025-09-10

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI appetite is real but under-activated: buyers pulled the conversation toward AI themselves and Marshall called Merlin 'exceptional' (7 Jul 2025), yet no AI/Merlin POC has been committed and the agentic tail-spend negotiation story has not been put back in front of the buyer since the deal regressed. The Merlin auto-negotiation narrative is Zycus's clearest wedge against Medix on Indirect.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 6.0 / Moderate. Calls corroborate genuine but cautious AI interest (autonomous negotiation and AP Smartdesk impressed attendees; Chris Bratt probed vendor AI openness), while budget is a protected number still being built and no AI POC is committed, so the field reads accurately rather than under- or over-rated.

items.detail: Economic buyer Jacky Xu (VP, CPO) has never engaged in 15+ months; deal is single-threaded to Chris Bratt for both the recommendation and the EB introduction.
Medix (Siemens Mendex) won Direct and Nexteer intends to use its supplier module for all direct/indirect suppliers, threatening to absorb the Indirect scope Zycus is counting on.
Eric Chavez frames Indirect as a year away, tied to the ERP/Direct replatform, creating a strong deferral / status-quo risk against the 20 Nov close.
Budget is a 'protected number for next year' still being built with no Indirect amount locked; the forecast was cut to Pipeline on 9 Jun 2026.
Scope narrowed from full S2P (Direct + Indirect) to Indirect only after Direct went to Medix; a defensive reduction that lowers deal value and win probability.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling. The last transcribed buyer working session was 14 Nov 2025; since then only a social lunch (14-15 May 2026) and a rep note of an internal blocker (12 Jun 2026), with the deal formally regressed on 9 Jun 2026.
items: No transcribed buyer working session since 2025-11-14.
Stage regressed and forecast cut on 2026-06-09.
12 Jun 2026 next step is an unresolved internal Nexteer blocker with no buyer reply since.

scope_change.from: Full Source-to-Pay across Direct + Indirect materials (combined single-scope pricing, ~$784k)
scope_change.detail: Direct materials went to Medix (Siemens Mendex) and Zycus self-disqualified on Direct (APQP/PPAP, Arthur 14 Nov 2025); the remaining Zycus play is Indirect, a defensive narrowing confirmed by the 12 Jun 2026 next step.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Nexteer is a stalled 15-month Qualified deal that just regressed from Shortlisted and lost Direct to Medix; the only path to the win is protecting the Indirect wedge with Merlin AI value and finally getting CPO Jacky Xu, who has never engaged, into the room.

win_position.text: We hold the modeled front-runner position on Indirect with a $4-5M year-one tail-spend case and two bought-in procurement champions, but the deal sits back at Qualified after the 9 Jun 2026 regression with no economic-buyer engagement.
Economic buyer Jacky Xu (VP, CPO) has never appeared on a call in 15+ months; Chris Bratt said on 10 Sep 2025 Jacky only engages after his team makes a recommendation, which has not happened.

Medix (Siemens Mendex) won Direct and Nexteer intends to use its supplier module 'for all direct/indirect suppliers' (15 May 2026 lunch note), threatening to absorb the Indirect scope before Zycus closes it.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum reversed on 9 Jun 2026 when Rick Taranek moved the stage Shortlisted to Qualified and cut the forecast Upside Key Deal to Pipeline, with no transcribed buyer working session since 14 Nov 2025.

The last buyer touch was a social lunch with Chris Bratt on 14-15 May 2026 ('great to catch-up'), and the 12 Jun 2026 note flags an unresolved internal Marshall/Chris sequencing conflict with no buyer reply since.

customer_commitment.text: Two champions are operationally bought in: Marshall Bonner drove the supplier/invoicing demos and user-count workshops through 2025 and called Merlin 'exceptional' (7 Jul 2025).

Commitment is soft: budget is a 'protected number for next year' still being built, no Indirect evaluation is dated, and Eric Chavez frames Indirect as 'a year away, tied to our ERP activities' (14 Nov 2025).

deal_risk.text: Direct is gone to Medix and Zycus self-disqualified on Direct (APQP/PPAP, 14 Nov 2025), shrinking the deal to Indirect only, a defensive scope reduction that drags win probability.
No economic-buyer access and a cooling champion pair leave the deal single-threaded to Chris Bratt for both the team recommendation and the EB introduction.

close_plan_concretizing.evidence: Stage regressed to Qualified and forecast cut to Pipeline on 9 Jun 2026; no dated Indirect evaluation plan exists.

executive_alignment.evidence: EB Jacky Xu never engaged in 15+ months; single-threaded to Chris Bratt.

competitor_pressure.evidence: Medix intends to extend its supplier module to all direct/indirect suppliers (15 May 2026).

customer_preference.evidence: No decisive Indirect lean toward Zycus: Nexteer selected Medix/Mendex for Direct and Eric Chavez placed Indirect 'a year away, tied to our ERP activities' (14 Nov 2025). Earlier AI enthusiasm (Marshall: Merlin 'exceptional', 7 Jul 2025) has decayed since the 9 Jun 2026 stage regression.

business_case.evidence: Zycus modeled ~$4-5M year-one tail-spend savings against ~$500M indirect spend (Arthur, 14 Nov 2025), but Nexteer never returned the regional spend extract, so the number is Zycus-owned, not buyer-validated.

items.action: Get Chris Bratt to align with the relevant stakeholder on Indirect and book an Indirect working session.
items.trigger: Next step records an unresolved Marshall/Chris sequencing miscommunication needing an internal meeting.
items.expected_effect: Unblocks the internal stall and restarts a working cadence, the prerequisite to re-qualifying the deal.

items.action: Show Nexteer why a Direct-materials supplier module cannot cover Indirect tail-spend and global standardization.
items.trigger: Buyer intends to use the Mendex supplier module for all direct/indirect suppliers.
items.expected_effect: Protects the Indirect scope from Medix encroachment and reasserts Zycus's differentiation.

items.action: Ask Marshall and April to return the regional spend extract to co-build the ROI model.
items.trigger: Arthur repeatedly requested the region-by-region spend extract that was never returned.
items.expected_effect: Converts the $4-5M savings from a Zycus model into a buyer-owned business case.

items.action: Put the Merlin agentic tail-spend negotiation narrative back in front of the Indirect buyers.
items.trigger: AI appetite is real (Merlin 'exceptional', 7 Jul 2025) but no AI POC is committed and positioning has gone quiet.
items.expected_effect: Reactivates the AI wedge that separates Zycus from Medix and lifts the AIS signal.

items.action: Secure Chris Bratt's introduction to Jacky Xu once the Indirect recommendation is ready.
items.trigger: Chris Bratt: Jacky engages only after the team makes a recommendation; no EB meeting in 15+ months.
items.expected_effect: Opens the economic buyer needed to fund the next-year budget and advance past Qualified.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — Direct materials went to Medix (Siemens Mendex) and Zycus self-disqualified on Direct (APQP/PPAP, Arthur 14 Nov 2025); the remaining Zycus play is Indirect, a d (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 10 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — Direct materials went to Medix (Siemens Mendex) and Zycus self-disqualified on Direct (APQP/PPAP, Arthur 14 Nov….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 50 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (10), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
